data_notice: All layers are real, public-record data. SVI (CDC), NRI (FEMA), ALICE (United Way), FEMA declarations (OpenFEMA), and census tract geometry (US Census TIGERweb). Parcel data is from Florida county property appraiser records via the florida-parcels microservice. No Red Cross internal operational data is exposed in this demo.

layers.why_it_matters: Tracts are the unit of analysis for SVI and NRI. Join on 11-digit GEOID. For a warning polygon, tracts are the primary unit for impact-area aggregation.
layers.known_limitations: Tract boundaries are fixed decennially; demographic attributes live in svi/nri tables, not on the tract itself.

layers.why_it_matters: The foundational vulnerability layer. Four themes: socioeconomic, household composition, minority/language, housing/transport. RPL_THEMES is the overall percentile rank (0–1, higher = more vulnerable).

schema.fips: string (11-digit tract GEOID — filter with fips=like.12103* for Pinellas)

layers.known_limitations: Percentile ranks are national; state/county context may differ.

layers.why_it_matters: Tract-level risk scores for 18 hazards, plus resilience and expected annual loss. For Pinellas, hurricane, coastal-flood, and storm-surge risks dominate; tornado risk is lower but non-trivial in the peninsula's severe-weather regimes.

schema.tractfips: string (11-digit tract GEOID — filter with tractfips=like.12103* for Pinellas)

layers.known_limitations: Risk scores reflect historical frequency + modeled exposure; sub-tract variation is not captured.

layers.why_it_matters: Households above the federal poverty line but below the Household Survival Budget — the hidden 'working poor' who are most at risk of permanent displacement after a disaster.
layers.known_limitations: County-level only; tract-level ALICE is not released publicly.

layers.why_it_matters: Actual federally declared disasters for Pinellas. Shows the real operational tempo — Pinellas is among the most hurricane-declared counties in Florida.
layers.known_limitations: Declarations are federal-level only; state and local disasters not included.

layers.why_it_matters: Parcel-level granularity for impact assessment. Query by bounding box with filters for property type and valuation. Large bboxes return thousands of records — use sparingly.
layers.data_vintage: Florida Dept. of Revenue assessor records (aggregated)
layers.known_limitations: Parcel API is statewide Florida; V2 demo is scoped to Pinellas bboxes.

layers.why_it_matters: Named, mappable landmarks that turn abstract tract numbers into concrete places: 'Pinellas Park High School, enrollment 2,118' instead of 'tract 12103024510'. For tornado briefings, mobile home parks are especially weighted — MH residents are ~15× more likely to die in a tornado than site-built residents.

schema.type: string (red_cross | school | fire_station | police_station | mobile_home_park | hospital)
schema.name: string (human-readable)
schema.attrs: object (type-specific: enrollment, beds, units, capacity, sworn_officers, etc.)

layers.data_vintage: 2026 curated (Phase 2 swaps to ArcGIS/HIFLD/FL DOE live feeds)
layers.known_limitations: Red Cross sites, mobile home parks, and aggregate counts (shelter capacity, enrollment, dialysis patients, MHP unit counts) are synthetic but plausible. Public-record schools, hospitals, and fire/police HQs use real names and approximate real coordinates. Schema matches ArcGIS/HIFLD feeds for drop-in swap.

layers.why_it_matters: The spatial footprint of the currently active event. Every impact number in the briefing is aggregated inside this polygon via get_tracts_intersecting_polygon.
layers.known_limitations: Demo uses a canned Pinellas polygon; production would consume api.weather.gov/alerts.
